coverage_note: Comprehensive analysis of 2024 ND 117 (DB id 19860, 94 lines). Compared full opinion text against authoritative PDF source using pdftotext -layout. All substantive content verified after fixes—opinion text becomes byte-identical to PDF source (normalized for page furniture). Five distinct error instances identified: 1 caption structure error, 3 ellipsis spacing errors (6 instances of spaced periods converted to compact form), 1 paragraph section ordering error."

Complete verification performed. Compared database text against authoritative PDF source (pdftotext extraction with layout preservation). All six substantive paragraphs verified byte-for-byte identical when whitespace (layout) is normalized. All citations (N.D.R.App.P. 28, 35.1(a)(8), 35.1(a)(2), 38), case names, party names, judge names, attorney names and locations, docket number (No. 20230387), disposition (AFFIRMED), and monetary amount ($1,625.00) confirmed correct. Special characters (curly quotes U+201C/U+201D, paragraph marker ¶) verified identical in both sources. No OCR errors, no transcription defects, no missing text detected. The only differences between DB and PDF are layout/formatting artifacts: (1) PDF has centered headers and right-aligned caption styling; (2) PDF has signature block on separate lines with indentation; (3) PDF includes page number (1) and form-feed character (0x0C) at end. These are expected handling of publication layout and are not content errors."

flags.location_quote: Greek letter Iota (Ι) appears as section header at line 29
flags.description: Line 29 contains a single Greek letter Iota (Ι) instead of Roman numeral I or traditional section break. This appears intentional (matching source markdown), but is an unusual character choice for a legal opinion section marker.

proposals.evidence: Malformed HTML entity with escaped '<' character. The '<' is incorrectly encoded as '&</sup>lt;' instead of being properly closed. The footnote is rendered cleanly in the PDF without this corruption.
Line 43 shows a lone 'T' as a section divider between paragraphs [¶1] and [¶2]. The PDF clearly shows a Roman numeral 'I'. The 'T' is an OCR misrecognition of the serif Roman numeral 'I'.

Line 56 shows Greek Pi (Π) as a section divider between [¶4] and [¶5]. The PDF shows plain 'II' (Roman numeral two). This is an OCR character misrecognition, likely from confusion with visual similarity of serif 'II' to the Greek character.
Line 74 shows Greek Alpha (Α) as a section divider between [¶8] and [¶9]. The PDF shows Roman numeral 'III'. The Greek character is an OCR substitution error.
Line 78 shows Cyrillic Be (В) as a section divider between [¶9] and [¶10]. The PDF shows 'IV' (Roman numeral four). This is a Cyrillic character substitution error from OCR processing.

Line 112 shows LaTeX markup ' $\mathsf{C}$ ' as a section divider. The PDF displays a plain 'C'. The LaTeX markup is a formatting artifact from OCR or PDF processing that failed to recognize and render the plain letter.

flags.location_quote: Line 128: 'Private property shall not be taken or damaged for public use without just compensation having been first made to, or paid into court for the owner . . . . No right of way shall be appropriated...'
flags.description: Spaced ellipsis '. . . .' appears in block quote of N.D. Const. art. I, § 16. PDF rendering shows spaced form, which matches the DB. Per instructions, ellipsis treatment should match source (ND uses compact '....' form, but this quote shows spaced variant). VERIFIED: PDF matches spaced form, so this is not an error—no fix needed.

flags.location_quote: identify the witness's 1 The autopsy report was not entered into the trial record... when the court did not base its evidentiary ruling on its review of the autopsy report. opinions and the bases and reasons for those opinions
flags.description: Footnote marker and footnote content embedded inline in paragraph 18. The text reads '...identify the witness's 1 The autopsy report was not entered into the trial record. After oral argument before this Court, the State filed the autopsy report with the district court. We decline to review the autopsy report to determine whether it satisfied the requirements of N.D.R.Crim.P. 16(a)(1)(F) when the court did not base its evidentiary ruling on its review of the autopsy report. opinions and the bases...' The footnote number '1' and its full content should be separated from the running text. The sentence should read '...identify the witness's opinions and the bases and reasons for those opinions.' This is a footnote handling/ingestion defect in the DB text.

proposals.evidence: The PDF source shows 'Rule 58 of the North Dakota Supreme Court Administrative Rules "addresses vexatious' on one line, followed by page break (page number '2'), then 'litigation."' on the next line. The OCR/ingestion process incorrectly inserted an 'A' between 'vexatious' and 'litigation', likely picking up a stray character or section header artifact from the PDF layout. The PDF clearly shows these two words belong together with no 'A' between them.

proposals.old_exact: is An award of costs under N.D.C.C. § 28-26-10 discretionary
proposals.new_exact: An award of costs under N.D.C.C. § 28-26-10 is discretionary
proposals.source: 2024ND85.pdf lines 328-329: 'An award of costs under N.D.C.C. § 28-26-10 is discretionary'
proposals.evidence: The PDF source shows the correct form across lines 328-329 with 'is' correctly positioned after '§ 28-26-10'. The DB has 'is' at the beginning ('is An award') and missing after '§ 28-26-10', creating a grammatically malformed sentence. This is a clear word order error in the ingestion process.
proposals.note: Word displacement error - the word 'is' appears at the beginning of the sentence instead of after the statute citation. Found in paragraph 19 discussing award of costs discretionary nature.

flags.location_quote: Between paragraphs 24 and 25 in DB; between paragraphs 24 and 25 in source PDF

In the authoritative PDF, 'III' is centered on its own line and introduces the final conclusion section, appearing before [¶12]. In the database, 'III' incorrectly appears after [¶12]. This is a structural/sequence error where the heading was displaced during OCR or ingestion. The fix restores the correct section organization.

coverage_note: Complete line-by-line and character-by-character comparison of database text against authoritative PDF. Removed known form-feed (0x0C) page break and running header page numbers (1-4) per ABSOLUTE RULES Section 8. All remaining differences are whitespace/layout only (line wrapping from fixed-width PDF column structure). One substantive error identified and proposed."

proposals.source: 2024ND81.pdf pdftotext extract; ¶4 ends with 'judgment dismissing Wilhoit with prejudice.' per source, orphaned 'judgment arguing' is ingestion artifact
proposals.note: ¶4: Orphaned phrase 'judgment arguing' erroneously placed at end of ¶4. Should be removed entirely. The text '...the clerk entered a judgment dismissing Wilhoit with prejudice.' is complete; the phrase 'judgment arguing' belongs at the start of ¶5 as part of 'moved for summary judgment arguing' but was misplaced during ingestion.

proposals.source: 2024ND81.pdf lines 70-71; correct text is 'Weatherford moved for summary judgment arguing Weatherford owed no duty'
proposals.note: ¶5: Scrambled text. DB reads 'moved Weatherford owed' but should be 'moved for summary judgment arguing Weatherford owed'. The phrase 'for summary judgment arguing' was split across the text.

proposals.source: 2024ND81.pdf line 73; text ends 'granting Weatherford's summary judgment motion.' with no 'for summary' suffix
proposals.note: ¶5: Orphaned text 'for summary' appears after 'motion.' as a dangling fragment. Removing it completes the sentence correctly: 'On October 21, 2021, the district court entered an order granting Weatherford's summary judgment motion.'

coverage_note: Full opinion body analyzed from [¶1] through [¶42]. Caption formatting differences (PDF layout justification) are expected artifacts and not flagged. All paragraph markers present and accounted for. After applying the three proposed fixes, the opinion text is byte-identical to the authoritative PDF source (after normalization)."

proposals.source: PDF lines 201-202: 'Hartson argues this was an impermissible modification of the crime, intrudes on the legislative branch, and undermines our conclusions in the aforementioned cases.'
proposals.evidence: The PDF pdftotext extraction shows 'intrudes\non the' at lines 201-202. The preposition 'on' is grammatically correct with 'intrudes' (one intrudes on something), whereas 'in' is incorrect.

proposals.source: PDF line 202-203: 'undermines our conclusions in the aforementioned cases.'
proposals.evidence: The PDF pdftotext extraction shows 'conclusions in the' at lines 202-203. The preposition 'in' is correct (conclusions regarding/in the cases), whereas 'on' is incorrect.

coverage_note: Full opinion scanned by OCR and structural comparison. Both errors are in paragraph 12 ([¶12]). No other substantive text discrepancies detected between DB and PDF source."
